clues.clue: Scrooge McDuck's grandnephews are Huey, Dewey and this third triplet.
Chip 'n Dale led a squad of pint-sized detectives in this 1989 series.
In 'TaleSpin,' this easygoing bear pilot flies a cargo plane called the Sea Duck.
Darkwing Duck's heroic catchphrase ends with the line 'Let's get' this.
'DuckTales' (1987) drew on comics by this Disney 'Duck Man' artist, surname Barks.
Danny Elfman wrote the bouncy 1989 main theme for this animated sitcom.
Mark Snow's eerie, lyric-free 'X-Files' theme is this kind of piece.
Ramin Djawadi's cello-driven main title scores this HBO fantasy epic.
Henry Mancini wrote this jazzy 1958 detective theme before scoring 'The Pink Panther.'
The early-electronic 'Doctor Who' theme was realized at the BBC's '___ Workshop' bearing this name.
This Midwestern state is the skeptical 'Show-Me State.'
With the motto 'Eureka,' this 'Golden State' joined the Union in 1850.
This northern state is the 'Land of 10,000 Lakes.'
This is the only state nicknamed the 'Beehive State.'
This 'Tar Heel State' takes its nickname from its Civil War soldiers.
In 'Goldfinger,' the villain plots to irradiate the gold inside this U.S. depository.
In 'Die Hard,' Hans Gruber's true aim is robbing bearer bonds from this L.A. tower.
In 'Avengers: Infinity War,' Thanos snaps to erase this fraction of all life.
In 'Despicable Me,' Gru's grand scheme is to steal this entire celestial body.
In 'The Dark Knight,' this villain's real plan is to corrupt Gotham's hero Harvey Dent.
This northern territory is the largest U.S. state by area.
Four states meet at a single point in the American Southwest known by this name.
Flowing from Montana, this is the longest river in the United States.
This is the only Great Lake lying entirely within the United States.
At about 20,310 feet, this Alaskan peak is the highest in North America.

final.clue: This stuttering Looney Tunes character closed many shorts with 'Th-th-that's all, folks!'
